title: Yen depreciates to 163 yen per dollar, its lowest level in 39 years
summary: The Japanese currency reached its weakest exchange rate since 1986, driven by buying of the dollar as a safe haven amid escalating tensions in the Middle East.

title: Extreme heat wave in Japan leaves at least four dead and record temperatures of 40°C
summary: Several regions of the country recorded temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius, while authorities warn that the intense heat will continue throughout the week.

title: Chinese warship fires machine guns inside Japan's exclusive economic zone
summary: The Japanese Ministry of Defense confirmed that a Chinese destroyer conducted shooting exercises in waters near Okinotori Island, which sparked diplomatic protests.

title: Canada joins as observer to the next-generation fighter jet development program between Japan, UK and Italy
summary: Canada's inclusion aims to reduce costs and expand the market for the joint fighter jet, according to official sources.

title: Japanese government defines its new economic and fiscal strategy in the 'Policy Skeleton'
summary: Takashi's cabinet approves the basic guidelines that include reduction of social insurance contributions, promotion of domestic investment and a change in fiscal management.

title: Prime Minister Takashi sparks controversy by revealing he sleeps between 0 and 3 hours daily
summary: Through his account on X, the Japanese leader shared details about his lack of sleep, which sparked mixed reactions in public opinion.

title: Yomiuri Giants achieve first victory with 10 runs and Yamazaki gets first win of the season
summary: Pitcher Yamazaki Iori earned his first win of the season in a game where the Giants scored 10 runs and achieved their third consecutive victory.

title: Daizen Maeda's goal is a candidate for best goal of the World Cup
summary: FIFA included the Japanese striker's shot among the nominees for the best goal of the World Cup award.

title: Television producer Yuriko Kobayashi, creator of successful dramas like 'Oshin', dies at 86
summary: The renowned NHK producer, responsible for iconic Japanese television series, died leaving a legacy in the entertainment industry.

title: 25 years after the Akashi pedestrian bridge tragedy, families remember the victims
summary: It has been 25 years since the accident in which 11 people were crushed on a bridge during a festival, and the father of a victim recounts the moments of pain.

title: Assets of Japanese lawmakers revealed; average exceeds 32 million yen
summary: The asset declaration of legislators shows that 27 own more than 100 million yen, while new lawmakers report an average of 13 million.
